lastOutput:
critical — without it, clicking the Abort button would also trigger the card's `onClick` and open the detail modal
- **`useState` for `aborting`** provides visual feedback during the async abort call, preventing double-clicks
- The button uses the same `accent-red` color system and `StopCircle` icon as the existing Abort button in the detail modal, keeping the visual language consistent
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

Done! Here's a summary of the change:

**What**: Added an Abort button directly on Kanban ticket cards for `in_progress` and `needs_approval` tickets.

**Why**: Previously you had to click into the detail modal to abort a running agent. Now you can stop it with one click from the board view — important when "sth goes wrong!"

**Single file changed**: `src/components/TicketCard.tsx` — added `handleAbort()` with `stopPropagation`, a `StopCircle` button with loading state, rendered for running tickets.

https://github.com/jess-bergs/agent-kanban/pull/46
